events.title: US Strikes Iranian Targets Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
events.desc: The US military conducted strikes on dozens of Iranian targets overnight, including missile sites and command centers, in response to Iran's targeting of American bases across the Gulf and attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. This escalation follows Iran's earlier claims of closing the strait and attacking US allies.

events.title: US Reinstates Blockade of Iranian Ports, Vows Control of Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: President Donald Trump announced the US is reinstating a blockade on Iranian ports, stating the US will "take over" the Strait of Hormuz and levy charges on shipping. This action comes amidst escalating tensions and exchanges of strikes between the US and Iran.

events.title: Iran-US Ceasefire Memorandum Frayed Amid Strait of Hormuz Disagreements
events.desc: The interim agreement between the US and Iran, signed in June, is rapidly deteriorating due to mutual claims of violations and disagreements over the Strait of Hormuz. The 14-point memorandum aimed to end the war and keep the strait open, but vagueness on key issues complicates efforts for lasting peace.

events.title: Yemen Government Forces Strike Houthi-Controlled Sanaa Airport
events.desc: Yemen's internationally recognized government forces announced they struck Sanaa airport, controlled by the Houthis, in what is described as the largest escalation in years. The Houthis blamed Saudi Arabia for the attack and threatened retaliation, while the government claimed the strike was to prevent an Iranian plane from landing.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Fire as Tensions Escalate Over Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: In a significant escalation, the US and Iran have engaged in reciprocal strikes. The US military reported hitting Iranian targets, while Iran claimed attacks on US military assets in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain, and Oman. This exchange occurred shortly after renewed hostilities involving shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: UK Plans to Ban Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)
events.desc: The UK government announced plans to designate Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization, citing its perceived threat to national security and its links to attacks on British soil. This move follows accusations of the IRGC's involvement in death threats and intimidation.

events.title: Iranian-Born Engineer Convicted in US for Exporting Drone Technology to Iran
events.desc: An Iranian-born engineer has been convicted in the United States on charges of conspiring to illegally export technology with potential applications in military drones to a company in Iran. This company reportedly supplied customers including Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC).

events.title: US Central Command Reports Hitting 90 Iranian Targets
events.desc: US Central Command stated it struck 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of retaliatory actions. The Iranian health ministry reported that 14 people were killed since Tuesday amid the ongoing exchanges of fire.

events.title: US Insists Strait of Hormuz is Open Amid Exchanges with Iran
events.desc: Despite Iran's claims that the Strait of Hormuz is closed and its launch of wide-ranging attacks on US allies and regional bases, US officials maintain that the vital waterway remains open. This follows incidents where tankers were hit in the strait.

events.title: Iraqi PM Heads to US Seeking Balance Between Security and Economy
events.desc: Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi is visiting the White House to seek significant US investment in Iraq's energy sectors. The talks are expected to focus on energy investment and the role of pro-Iran groups, aiming to balance security needs with economic recovery following regional instability.

events.title: EU Sanctions Nine Individuals Over Alleged Russian Cyber-Espionage
events.desc: The European Union has imposed sanctions on nine individuals and four entities for their alleged involvement in a long-standing cyber-espionage campaign. The move highlights ongoing concerns about state-sponsored cyber operations targeting EU member states.

events.title: Yemen Government Claims Responsibility for Sanaa Airport Attack
events.desc: Yemeni government forces claimed responsibility for striking Sanaa International Airport, aiming to prevent an Iranian plane from landing. The Houthis, however, accused Saudi Arabia, the government's ally, of carrying out the strikes and threatened retaliation, escalating the dormant conflict.

events.title: US Imposes New Sanctions on Cuba Tourism Ministry and State Companies
events.desc: The US Treasury Department has implemented new sanctions targeting Cuba's tourism ministry and state-owned companies. This action is part of the ongoing US policy towards Cuba and aims to exert further economic pressure.

events.title: EU Unveils $1 Billion Fund for Gaza War Recovery
events.desc: The European Union has announced a $1 billion fund dedicated to the recovery efforts in Gaza following the recent conflict. However, this amount falls significantly short of the estimated $71 billion needed for Gaza's recovery over the next decade.

events.title: US Democrat Ro Khanna Detained by Armed Israeli Settlers
events.desc: US Representative Ro Khanna reported being detained for 90 minutes by armed Israeli settlers while visiting the occupied West Bank. The incident highlights ongoing tensions and security concerns in the region.
